name: Guyana
avoid: May–August and December–January are the wettest stretches, when the Linden–Lethem road gets rougher, slower, and much more tiring.
Kaieteur Falls flights are weather dependent and often need minimum passenger loads, so do not build a same-day international connection around them.
Domestic planes and charters in Guyana often enforce soft-bag and light-luggage limits, especially on Rupununi sectors.
stopoverNote: Most India routings reach Georgetown via New York, Miami, Toronto, or a Caribbean hub; keep Kaieteur flexible until your Georgetown arrival is locked because weather and load factors matter.

cities.name: Georgetown
cities.notes: Arrival base for colonial architecture, markets, museums, seawall evenings, and the easiest setup for Kaieteur flights or southbound road-and-air transfers.

cities.name: Kaieteur Falls
cities.notes: Usually done as a same-day scenic flight from Georgetown for one of the world's tallest single-drop waterfalls and the classic Guyana wildlife-and-geology highlight.

cities.name: Iwokrama Rainforest
cities.notes: Guyana's great forest corridor for Atta's canopy walkway, the Kurupukari crossing, river-lodge wildlife, and deep overland rainforest travel.

cities.name: Rupununi Savannah
cities.notes: Wide-open grassland and wetland country around Annai, Yupukari, Karanambu, and Lethem, best known for giant anteaters, lodges, ranch stays, and black-caiman nights.

itinerary.sim: Digicel or GTT prepaid works best for arrival logistics in Georgetown; budget roughly ₹1,200–₹2,500 for a short-stay data pack, then expect patchy or no signal once you are deep in Iwokrama or the Rupununi.

Georgetown.name: Georgetown
Georgetown.note: Stay central or near the seawall so you have easier restaurant options, simpler airport transfers to Ogle, and a calmer buffer before the long interior stretches.

days.theme: Cathedral woodwork, museums, and Georgetown's classic food-and-seawall rhythm

activities.name: St. George's Cathedral visit and nearby heritage walk
Guyana National Museum
Promenade Gardens, State House area, and Georgetown Seawall stroll
Dinner at Backyard Cafe or The Hibiscus Restaurant

days.theme: Markets, anthropology, and a practical setup day for flights inland

activities.name: Stabroek Market and waterfront walk
Walter Roth Museum of Anthropology
Bourda Market snack crawl
Taxi transfer between central Georgetown and Eugene F. Correia Airport

Kaieteur Falls.name: Kaieteur Falls
Kaieteur Falls.note: Treat this as a weather-sensitive day trip from Georgetown; confirm the flight the evening before and travel with only a light day bag.

days.theme: Guyana's signature scenic flight and waterfall day

activities.name: Trans Guyana Airways or Evergreen Adventures Kaieteur Falls day flight from Ogle
activities.tip: A current public benchmark is about US$350 per person including the return flight and guided stop at the falls.

activities.name: Guided Kaieteur viewpoints including Boy Scout Point, Johnson's View, and the main escarpment edge
Kaieteur National Park ranger talk on the golden rocket frog and bromeliad habitat
Light snack or boxed lunch around the airstrip stop

Iwokrama Rainforest.name: Iwokrama Rainforest
Iwokrama Rainforest.note: Base either at Atta for the walkway or at Iwokrama River Lodge for river access; both work best if you carry cash, a torch, and patience for road or ferry delays.

days.theme: Interior road journey, Kurupukari crossing, and first canopy views

activities.name: Georgetown to Iwokrama shared minibus or 4x4 over the Linden–Lethem Road with Kurupukari river crossing
Iwokrama Canopy Walkway
activities.tip: A current published benchmark is about US$14.95 per visitor.

activities.name: Atta Rainforest trails and late-afternoon birding
activities.cost: Included with lodge stay or ₹1,500–₹3,000 pp guided

activities.name: Optional night drive or spotlight walk near Atta

days.theme: Essequibo wildlife, Turtle Mountain, and deeper forest time

activities.name: Dawn birding from the Atta or Iwokrama trail network
activities.cost: Included with lodge stay or ₹1,500–₹3,000 pp guided

activities.name: Iwokrama River Lodge boat trip on the Essequibo
Turtle Mountain guided hike
Kurupukari river-boat crossings and local transfers

days.theme: Extra canopy dawn, Surama-side transition, and a slower forest morning

activities.name: Second sunrise canopy session at Atta
Fair View–Surama road transfer deeper into North Rupununi
Surama village nature walk
Downtime for hammock, birding, or lodge-led wildlife spotting

Rupununi Savannah.name: Rupununi Savannah
Rupununi Savannah.note: Choose one main base around Yupukari, Karanambu, Annai, or Lethem rather than trying to move every night; distances are long and wildlife timing matters more than ticking off too many lodges.

days.theme: Savannah transfer, wetlands, and your first wide-open Rupununi evening

activities.name: 4x4 or minibus transfer from Iwokrama to Annai, Karanambu, or Yupukari
Surama village walk or Annai foothill trail
Karanambu wetlands boat or giant anteater outing
Sunset savannah wildlife drive

days.theme: Yupukari conservation day and black-caiman night

activities.name: Caiman House wildlife-adventure package with night river research, transport from Lethem, meals, activities, and accommodation
activities.tip: Visit Rupununi currently lists this all-inclusive package at GYD 210,000.

activities.name: Night Rupununi River boat with the black caiman research team
Yupukari wetland birding and village downtime
Optional turtle or river-edge interpretation with local guides

days.theme: Lethem frontier day, ranch culture, and the easiest exit south or back to Georgetown

activities.name: Shared minibus into Lethem or onward lodge transfer
Lethem market, Takutu River Bridge, and border-town browsing
Waikin Ranch horseback or ranch excursion
Trans Guyana Airways Lethem–Georgetown domestic flight or comparable charter-sector benchmark

days.theme: Optional extra lodge day for anteaters, river life, or a slower savannah finish

activities.name: Second Karanambu or Yupukari wildlife outing
Rupununi River boat or oxbow-lake birding
Craft purchases and village guiding tips
Buffer day for weather, road conditions, or charter timing

connections.method: Trans Guyana Airways or another sightseeing flight from Eugene F. Correia Airport, usually about 1 hour each way
Return by air to Georgetown, then continue by minibus or 4x4 over the Linden–Lethem Road and Kurupukari crossing; private or charter options are faster but pricier
connections.cost: ₹4,000–₹8,000 pp by road after the return flight / ₹12,000+ pp if using an interior flight sector

connections.method: Continue south by 4x4, minibus, lodge transfer, or a light-aircraft hop toward Annai or Lethem depending your lodge
connections.cost: ₹2,000–₹4,500 pp by road / ₹12,000–₹22,000 pp by domestic flight

extras: Carry more cash than usual once you leave Georgetown; cards are inconsistent in the interior and many lodges or transport fixes still settle in cash.
Ask every lodge about baggage limits before booking a Trans Guyana Airways sector or charter, especially if you are carrying binoculars, camera gear, or hard suitcases.
Kaieteur works best either at the start or end of the trip, because weather cancellations are easier to absorb when you still have a Georgetown buffer.
Road time in Guyana is highly seasonal: the same Linden–Lethem section can feel straightforward in dry weather and exhausting after rain.
Interior nights are darker, quieter, and more insect-heavy than most travelers expect, so carry a torch, quick-dry layers, and repellent even if your lodge looks polished online.
